name: Totoya Kaimuki
tagline: Sixteen-seat kaisendon counter where the sashimi is piled so thick you can barely see the rice — get there early or join the wait.
mustOrder: kaisen don — the signature sashimi rice bowl with daily fish selection
negitori don — torched salmon belly over rice
miso soup — yuzu or ginger-infused, not an afterthought
parking: Street parking on 12th Ave and side streets; competitive during lunch rush
talkStory: Opened late 2024 on 12th Ave in Kaimuki and immediately became one of the hardest lunch seats on the island. Only 16 counter seats with an open kitchen — you watch your bowl being built. The fish quality is exceptional, sourced daily. Lines form before opening. The move is to come at an off-peak time (weekday, 1:30pm or late dinner). 4.8 Google stars with 200+ reviews — one of the highest-rated new restaurants on Oahu. Daily 11am–2:30pm and 5pm–8:30pm.
